calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-26-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Jason Burrows of Winn Law Group, Tel. No. 1-714-446-6686 appearing for
the Plaintiff by telephone appearance;
Atty. Angela Kaaiahelo, tel. No. 1-646-379-6414 appearing for the Defendant.


RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Dennis Bullock's motion to set aside and vacate renewal of judgment is GRANTED. As Bullock seeks to vacate renewal of the judgment under CCP 683.170, he is not seeking equitable relief in a collateral proceeding and the limitations periods for CCP 473 and 473.5 do not apply. Failure to serve process is a ground to vacate renewal of a judgment, and Bullock filed this motion within the 30-day statutory period. (See Fidelity Creditor Service, Inc. v. Browne (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 195, 200-02.) Bullock says he did not get actual notice until he received the notice of renewed judgment, but even assuming Bullock had notice when his wages were first garnished, "actual knowledge of the judgment [does] not govern the timeliness of his section 683.170 motion." (Id. at 204.) A 683.170 motion requires no showing of diligence or a meritorious defense in the underlying action. (Id. at 203-06.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/ru
